aggregateSignals.title: Focused single-persona developer update pages achieve first-attempt acceptance
aggregateSignals.evidence: 3/3 first-attempt successes (TASK-004, TASK-006, TASK-008) share: action=update, contentType=howto, targetPersonas=[developer], estimatedComplexity=medium, sectionsModify≥2, sectionsAdd≤1. Average invariants applied: 5.7.
aggregateSignals.recommendation: When planning update tasks for single-persona developer howto pages with medium complexity, expect high first-attempt success. Prioritize these tasks early to build pipeline momentum.

aggregateSignals.title: Create-action pages have higher rejection rates than updates
aggregateSignals.evidence: Create tasks (TASK-001, TASK-002) averaged 2.5 attempts vs update tasks averaged 1.9 attempts. Create tasks triggered frontmatter (INV-frontmatter-004), cross-ref (INV-crossref-001), and structural (INV-structure-001) rejections that don't apply to updates.
aggregateSignals.recommendation: Create-action tasks need explicit writer briefing on: redirect_from derivation from identifier, required page sections (Title/Intro/Body/Result/Next Steps), and page_link identifier verification.

aggregateSignals.title: Style invariant INV-formatting-016 (em dash vs en dash) is the most frequent single rejection cause
aggregateSignals.evidence: INV-formatting-016 triggered rejections in 3 tasks (TASK-007, TASK-010, TASK-014). Writers consistently produce em dashes (—) when the style guide requires en dashes (–).
aggregateSignals.recommendation: Add explicit em-dash/en-dash guidance to the content writer's pre-writing checklist. Consider a post-write automated check before human review.

aggregateSignals.title: High invariant count correlates with first-attempt success for update tasks
aggregateSignals.evidence: First-attempt tasks applied more invariants on average (5.7 invariants) vs two-cycle update tasks. TASK-008 applied 11 invariants and succeeded first try. TASK-010 applied 13 invariants and needed only standard 2 cycles.
aggregateSignals.recommendation: Encourage writers to explicitly apply more invariants upfront rather than relying on reviewer feedback.

aggregateSignals.title: Business-user pages face unique accuracy challenges around UI behavior
aggregateSignals.evidence: TASK-011 required 3 cycles because the writer described a UI field interaction incorrectly (manual code name entry vs auto-generated field). TASK-012 and TASK-013 both had pre-existing icon errors and terminology corrections. Business-user content accuracy depends on UI verification.
aggregateSignals.recommendation: Business-user task briefings should include explicit UI form field metadata (auto-generated fields, collapsed sections, field labels from resource files).

aggregateSignals.title: Research recommendations improve coverage but don't prevent style rejections
aggregateSignals.evidence: Tasks citing more research recommendations (TASK-001: 7, TASK-002: 8, TASK-009: 4) still required multiple cycles due to style invariant violations. Research recommendations improve content completeness but don't address formatting and structural invariants.
aggregateSignals.recommendation: Research recommendations and style invariants serve orthogonal purposes. Both must be addressed independently.

styleEvolutions.observation: Em dash (—) vs en dash (–) enforcement triggered rejections in 3 tasks (TASK-007, TASK-010, TASK-014)
styleEvolutions.description: Writers consistently produce em dashes when en dashes are required. INV-formatting-016 is well-defined but writers need stronger pre-writing guidance on dash types.

styleEvolutions.observation: Callout bold title requirement (INV-callout-002) triggered rejections in 3 tasks (TASK-001, TASK-009, TASK-015)
styleEvolutions.description: Multi-sentence callouts must have a bold title on the first line. Writers omit this consistently — suggests the invariant needs more prominent placement in writer briefings.

styleEvolutions.observation: Empty redirect_from frontmatter triggered rejections in 2 tasks (TASK-001, TASK-002)
styleEvolutions.description: Create-action pages consistently get empty redirect_from arrays. Writers need explicit guidance to derive redirect_from from identifier pattern (x/<identifier>).

styleEvolutions.observation: Incorrect page_link identifiers triggered rejections in 2 tasks (TASK-002, TASK-017)
styleEvolutions.description: Writers fabricate page_link identifiers (e.g., 'member_roles_developer_guide_xp' instead of 'member_roles_xp'). Cross-reference accuracy requires the writer to verify identifiers against actual front matter.

styleEvolutions.observation: Prohibited interaction verbs (choose/enable) enforced in 2 business-user tasks (TASK-012, TASK-013)
styleEvolutions.description: Business-user pages consistently use 'choose' and 'enable/disable' instead of the required 'select' and 'clear'. Xperience style guide mandates select/clear for checkbox interactions.

styleEvolutions.observation: Pre-existing icon errors (xp-lock → xp-diamond) discovered and fixed in 2 tasks (TASK-012, TASK-013)
styleEvolutions.description: Accuracy reviewer discovered stale lock icons in pre-existing content within modified scope. The icon changed from lock to diamond for secured content status. Accuracy reviewers catch pre-existing errors well.

styleEvolutions.observation: Unicode arrow (→) vs ASCII arrow (->) inconsistency noted in style reviews for 2+ tasks (TASK-006, TASK-012)
styleEvolutions.description: INV-style-046 requires ASCII '->' but existing codebase predominantly uses Unicode '→'. Style reviewers note this as acceptable deviation. Candidate for invariant revision.
